work_unit: fix scout-scan negative-score bug: pend_triage flat -1 causes projects with real TODO debt to score negative and be hidden behind score-0 healthy projects
situation: scout-scan recommendations showed all 7 projects at score=0.0 with 'no clear work unit'. But moni-master (12 TODOs) scored -0.94 and x-tool (5 TODOs) scored -0.97 — both hidden outside top-3. Root cause: `score -= 1` on line 395 subtracted a flat 1 from the total score when a pending triage worktree existed. For large repos (2000 files), marker density is tiny (0.006 for 12 TODOs) → density contribution ~0.06, then -1 = -0.94. The flat subtraction was orders of magnitude larger than the contribution it intended to cancel.
action: Replaced `score -= 1` with `score -= min(density * 10, 5) + min((m['FIXME'] + m['HACK']) * 0.2, 4)` — this precisely cancels the two marker-related score additions (density and FIXME/HACK weighting) without going negative. Projects with pending triage now score 0.0 (same as healthy projects, not below them). Applied to both live copy (workspace/scripts/) and git-tracked copy (autonomous-studio/scripts/).
outcome_evidence: Before fix: moni=-0.94, x-tool=-0.97 (hidden). After fix: both=0.00 (visible in recommendations). Verified via --json output comparing full score lists.
next_suggestion: All projects now score 0.0 — all healthy with pending worktrees. Next useful work: merge pending worktrees (opt-moni-1782561370, opt-x-tool-1782563095, opt-planning-1782557428) to land triage fixes, or look for new non-triage work items in any project.
